quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 11 Quiz 1
quiz.subtitle: 11.1: Angles and Radian Measure
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Introducing angle measurement using radians and conversions

questions.question: Convert $150^{\circ}$ to radians in terms of $\pi$.
Which of the following angles is coterminal with $-60^{\circ}$?
questions.explanation: Coterminal angles are found by adding or subtracting multiples of $360^{\circ}$. $-60^{\circ} + 360^{\circ} = 300^{\circ}$.

questions.question: Identify the coterminal angle for $\frac{11\pi}{4}$ that lies in the interval $[0, 2\pi)$.
Find a positive angle less than $2\pi$ that is coterminal with $\frac{17\pi}{3}$.
Find the reference angle for $225^{\circ}$.
questions.explanation: 225° is in Quadrant III. Reference angle = $225^{\circ} - 180^{\circ} = 45^{\circ}$.

questions.question: Find the arc length of a circle with radius 10 inches subtended by a central angle of $\frac{\pi}{4}$ radians.
questions.explanation: $s = r\theta = 10 \times \frac{\pi}{4} = \frac{10\pi}{4} = \frac{5\pi}{2}$ inches. Arc length formula uses radians.

questions.question: Find the degree measure of an angle that is $\frac{1}{6}$ of a full counter-clockwise rotation.
An angle measures $\frac{\pi}{10}$ radians. What is this in degrees?

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You are speaking the language of radians — converting between degrees and radians, finding coterminal angles in both systems, determining reference angles, and computing arc length.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Key reminders: (1) Degrees to radians: multiply by $\frac{\pi}{180}$, (2) Radians to degrees: multiply by $\frac{180}{\pi}$, (3) Coterminal: add or subtract $360^{\circ}$ or $2\pi$, (4) Arc length $s = r\theta$ only works with $\theta$ in radians.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: (1) Simplifying fractions when converting, (2) Subtracting $2\pi$ enough times to get into $[0, 2\pi)$, (3) Reference angle: QI = angle, QII = $180^{\circ}-$angle, QIII = angle$-180^{\circ}$, QIV = $360^{\circ}-$angle.
scoreMessages.0: Review radian basics: (1) $\pi$ radians = $180^{\circ}$, $2\pi$ radians = $360^{\circ}$, (2) To convert degrees $\to$ radians: $\times \frac{\pi}{180}$, (3) To convert radians $\to$ degrees: $\times \frac{180}{\pi}$, (4) Coterminal angles differ by $360^{\circ}$ or $2\pi$ radians.
